currentPhase.name: Parallel push-readiness conversion against locked baseline
currentPhase.summary: The product flow is approved and archived as complete, `/api/v1` is the active backend contract baseline, and the company is converting ready source artifacts into repo-readiness, dashboard-prep, and release-prep progress in parallel while CTO-owned canonical viz publication remains an external dependency rather than the central gate.

project.name: Pika
project.purpose: Pika helps groups rank choices fast through playful head-to-head matchups, then share the result.

contract.policy: Backend defines canonical v1 slice contract; frontend conforms after publication.
contract.frontendWiringNote: Frontend integration against the backend-owned contract is the next engineering gate after backend artifact/package acceptance advances.

phaseTransitionArchive.note: COO approved this archive snapshot as the current retrievable reference for the restored parallel-work phase transition.

readySourceArtifacts.name: Approved product baseline flow
readySourceArtifacts.evidence: Product-flow approval is archived complete and treated as stable for additive refinement only.
readySourceArtifacts.conversionUse: Supports comprehension/polish refinement work and preserves a locked sequence for engineering and launch-prep alignment.

readySourceArtifacts.name: Backend v1 slice contract baseline
readySourceArtifacts.evidence: `/api/v1` is approved as the active backend namespace and workspace/packages/contracts/pika-vertical-slice.json is the canonical contract path.
readySourceArtifacts.conversionUse: Provides the integration target for frontend wiring and the baseline for downstream readiness verification once package completeness is accepted.

readySourceArtifacts.name: Push-readiness reporting references
readySourceArtifacts.evidence: archive/push-readiness-checklist.md, archive/push-readiness-status.md, and archive/operating-health-reference.md are already accepted references.
readySourceArtifacts.conversionUse: Supports dashboard-prep clarification, repo-readiness ordering, and artifact-linked support verification without waiting on publication.

readySourceArtifacts.name: Phase-transition archive snapshot
readySourceArtifacts.evidence: archive/2026-04-29-phase-transition.md was created and approved by COO as the current retrievable transition reference.
readySourceArtifacts.conversionUse: Makes the restored parallel-work phase and blocker chain explicit for CEO visibility and ongoing operating interpretation.

readySourceArtifacts.name: Verified frontend preview deployment configuration
readySourceArtifacts.evidence: Current support activation state notes frontend preview deployment config already verified.
readySourceArtifacts.conversionUse: Allows release-prep and deployment-readiness follow-through to continue once backend package/env verification is ready.

remainingConversionBlockers.name: Backend package completeness and sign-off
remainingConversionBlockers.impact: Blocks clean handoff into accepted frontend contract-backed integration and downstream verification.
remainingConversionBlockers.evidence: Backend produced artifacts, but CTO has not granted sign-off because the escalation package contents were incomplete.

remainingConversionBlockers.name: Frontend integration against accepted backend baseline
remainingConversionBlockers.impact: Blocks integrated behavior validation against live backend responses and keeps readiness conversion short of a full connected slice.
remainingConversionBlockers.evidence: Frontend progress exists, but the dashboard still treats full contract-backed integration as the next engineering gate.

remainingConversionBlockers.name: Canonical viz publication to companies/Pika/viz.json
remainingConversionBlockers.impact: Blocks canonical published dashboard visibility, but should not block parallel repo-readiness, dashboard-prep, archive, or release-prep work.
remainingConversionBlockers.evidence: CEO explicitly reopened parallel work while keeping CTO ownership of publication.

nextConcreteHandoffs.handoff: Move backend from artifacts-landed to accepted package completeness, then hand the approved baseline into frontend contract-backed integration against workspace/packages/contracts/pika-vertical-slice.json.
Use the locked baseline flow to dispatch comprehension-gap, empty-state, and polish refinements that do not alter core sequencing.
Convert the approved baseline into waitlist/referral, share-loop, and launch-message assets so release-facing packaging is ready when engineering verification clears.
Use the accepted readiness references and approved phase-transition archive to clarify backlog requirements, conversion blockers, and artifact ordering outside the publication dependency.
Stay conditional and activate only when concrete CI, deployment, archival, dashboard-prep, or publication-linked artifacts are ready for validation.

actions: Immediate post-prototype integration sequencing is in force: completed prototype and baseline approvals must roll directly into backend acceptance, frontend contract wiring, repo-readiness artifact completion, and deployment/readiness verification.
Each executive stream must show one explicit next milestone or decision gate so completed work never sits without a named handoff.

Support-team activation is conditional and should only be treated as active when tied to concrete readiness artifacts such as CI validation, deployment config verification, backend env/package artifacts, dashboard-prep inputs, release-prep inputs, or canonical viz publication.

baselineStreams.name: engineering/build
baselineStreams.summary: Engineering is in immediate post-prototype integration mode. `/api/v1` is approved as the active backend baseline, backend produced artifacts but still lacks accepted package contents for final sign-off, and frontend hardening plus contract-backed integration can continue in parallel with adjacent repo-readiness work.
baselineStreams.nextMilestoneOrGate: Advance backend package acceptance/sign-off enough to clear the handoff into frontend contract-backed integration against workspace/packages/contracts/pika-vertical-slice.json, while continuing implementation hardening that does not depend on publication.

integrationSequencing.label: Prototype and product-flow approval
integrationSequencing.handoff: Roll forward immediately into backend baseline acceptance and integration work.

integrationSequencing.label: Backend `/api/v1` baseline and deployment/package artifacts
integrationSequencing.handoff: Resolve package completeness and sign-off so frontend can integrate against the accepted backend baseline without ambiguity.

integrationSequencing.label: Frontend contract wiring and integrated behavior
integrationSequencing.handoff: Wire the frontend to workspace/packages/contracts/pika-vertical-slice.json and validate locked-flow behavior against live backend responses.

integrationSequencing.label: Deployment/readiness verification
integrationSequencing.handoff: Use verified backend/frontend artifacts to close devops/platform readiness checks.

substreams.name: backend
substreams.note: Board shows backend task completion and `/api/v1` approval, but CTO still says sign-off is not granted because the escalation package contents were incomplete.

substreams.name: frontend
substreams.note: Frontend has progress on wiring shared client/async states, and contract-conformant hardening can continue while full integration remains the next engineering gate.

baselineStreams.name: prototype clarity
baselineStreams.summary: Product-flow approval is archived complete and no longer an active readiness gate. Product involvement is now parallel refinement work focused on comprehension gaps, empty states, and polish opportunities that do not change the approved core sequencing.
baselineStreams.nextMilestoneOrGate: Review the approved baseline flow for comprehension, empty-state, and polish refinements that can be dispatched without reopening the locked sequence.

baselineStreams.name: launch/live monitoring
baselineStreams.summary: Growth remains active and can prepare launch/share-loop packaging, waitlist/referral assets, launch messaging, and monitoring inputs in parallel while engineering clears the remaining acceptance and integration gates.
baselineStreams.nextMilestoneOrGate: Prepare release-prep and share-loop assets now so they can activate immediately once verified backend/frontend readiness artifacts are in hand.

baselineStreams.name: dashboard/repo readiness
baselineStreams.summary: Dashboard and repo readiness remain tied to the accepted operating-health reference and push-readiness records. Canonical viz publication is still a scoped dependency, but readiness ordering, blocker clarification, dashboard backlog prep, and support-team coordination can proceed now against concrete artifacts.
baselineStreams.nextMilestoneOrGate: Clarify the dashboard requirements/conversion-blocker backlog, keep readiness state aligned to archive/push-readiness-status.md, and complete artifact-linked repo/readiness prep that does not depend on canonical viz publication.

immediateDecisionGates.name: Product-flow approval
immediateDecisionGates.summary: Archived as complete; not an active gate.

immediateDecisionGates.name: Backend package completeness and sign-off
immediateDecisionGates.summary: Backend produced work, but acceptance/sign-off is still pending because CTO says the escalation package contents were incomplete.

immediateDecisionGates.name: Frontend integration against accepted backend baseline
immediateDecisionGates.summary: Next engineering gate: wire frontend behavior to the approved `/api/v1` backend baseline through workspace/packages/contracts/pika-vertical-slice.json.

immediateDecisionGates.name: Artifact-linked support verification
immediateDecisionGates.summary: Activate only when concrete readiness artifacts require validation, dashboard-prep clarification, release-prep support, publication, or archival state advancement.

supportTeamActivation.activateWhen: CI validation, dashboard-prep dependency clarification, or canonical viz publication is required.
Deployment config, release-prep inputs, or backend env/package artifacts are ready for verification.
supportTeamActivation.currentArtifactLink: Frontend preview deployment config already verified; remain ready for backend package/env verification and release-prep handoff.

supportTeamActivation.activateWhen: Readiness ledger/status note, dashboard-prep clarification, or canonical operating references require confirmation.

divergenceNotes.detail: Backend reports completed artifacts landed, but CTO has not granted sign-off because the escalation package still lacked actual contents.
Frontend implementation progress exists, but the dashboard still treats full contract-backed integration as the next engineering gate.
Support teams should not appear as continuously active unless they are tied to concrete readiness artifacts.

Canonical viz publication to companies/Pika/viz.json remains CTO-owned and outstanding, but it should be tracked as a scoped dependency while repo-readiness, dashboard-prep, release-prep, and archive-backed operating visibility continue in parallel.
